title: hierochloe glabra subsp. sachalinensis
intro: Hierochloe glabra subsp. sachalinensis, also known as sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to Japan and other parts of Asia. It is a member of the Poaceae family and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of sweet grass include soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Sweet grass is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
